replication_study.hypothesis: In a task-switching paradigm with monetary reward manipulation, the three-way interaction Trial type (switch vs. repeat) × Reward level (high vs. low) × Need for Cognition (NFC) on log-transformed reaction times is significant and positive for the interaction term, such that higher rewards reduce switch costs more for low-NFC individuals and reduce them less (or increase them) for high-NFC individuals.

data_plan.source_type: Laboratory computerized cognitive tasks with questionnaires (trial-level data; within-subject reward manipulation)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Two within-subject reward blocks (High vs Low reward) in task-switching; restrict analyses to correct trials for RT models; include both blocks and all participants with NFC and required variables present.
data_plan.sample_size: 35560 trials from 127 participants (trial-level dataset; 127 unique SubjectID values)
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Trial-level responses nested within participants (mixed-effects models with participant random intercepts).
data_plan.access_details: Provided locally in replication_data/sandra_replicate.csv. For containerized execution, place a copy at /app/data/sandra_replicate.csv.

qualification.explanation: This dataset implements the same experimental paradigm (Stroop + task-switching with monetary reward manipulation) and the same self-report measures (NFC, BIS/BAS), enabling a test of the identical interaction using independent data. The larger, independent sample provides a robust validation of the focal effect under similar conditions.
qualification.similarity_to_original: The original paper (original_paper.pdf, Table 3; post_registration.json) analyzed mixed-effects regressions with predictors: trial type (switch vs repeat), reward level, block, and NFC on log RTs, focusing on the Trial × Reward × NFC interaction. The replication dataset includes the same variables and coding: trial (−1 repeat, 1 switch), rewardlevel (−1 low, 1 high), blocknumber (contrast-coded), NFC, SubjectID, accuracy, logRT (replication_data/sandra_replicate_csv_codebook.pdf). The analysis syntax PDF shows the exact focal model formula using lme4 (replication_data/analysis syntax_Sandra_Cognition_2018_0qar - Molden - Direct Replication - 20g.pdf).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original sample size: 54 recruited, 45 analyzed, NFC analyses on 43 participants (post_registration.json, original_paper.pdf Methods). Replication dataset has 127 participants and 35,560 trials (dataset info). Recruitment source/platform may differ (not specified in codebook), implying an independent sample. Block coding differs in range (blocknumber contrast-coded from −5 to 5 here), but functionally matches original practice/block predictor (original_paper.pdf Methods).

data_plan.notes: Key variables present and correctly coded: SubjectID, logRT (ln of latency), accuracy (0/1), trial (−1/1), rewardlevel (−1/1), blocknumber (contrast-coded), NFC. 254 rows have missing 'trial' and will be dropped by complete cases. RT models should subset to accuracy==1, per original. The provided R syntax assumes a preloaded data.frame named sandra_replicate; a runnable script must instead read /app/data/sandra_replicate.csv. File encodings appear standard; data are long format.

steps: Subset to correct trials (accuracy == 1) for RT models.
Ensure complete cases on logRT, trial, rewardlevel, blocknumber, NFC, SubjectID.
Fit mixed-effects linear model on log RTs: scale(logRT) ~ scale(NFC) * trial * rewardlevel + blocknumber + (1|SubjectID).
Extract and record the coefficient, SE, t, and p-value for the Trial × Reward × NFC interaction; confirm positive sign.
Optionally fit mixed-effects logistic model on accuracy: accuracy ~ scale(NFC) * trial * rewardlevel + blocknumber + (1|SubjectID), with robust optimizer control, as a supplementary check.
Save summaries and fixed-effects tables to /app/data outputs.
planned_method.models: Mixed-effects linear regression (lme4::lmer) for log RTs with random intercepts for SubjectID; optional mixed-effects logistic regression (lme4::glmer) for accuracy.
planned_method.outcome_variable: logRT (natural log of reaction time), scaled for model fit.
planned_method.independent_variables: trial (−1 repeat, 1 switch), rewardlevel (−1 low, 1 high), NFC (mean score; scaled in model), blocknumber (contrast-coded linear block term).
planned_method.control_variables: blocknumber (practice effects); participant random intercept (1|SubjectID). Optional: BAS as covariate in robustness checks, mirroring original.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Fixed-effect coefficient for the Trial × Reward × NFC interaction term on scale(logRT).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald t-test from lmerTest summary (Type III ANOVA table optional for confirmation).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion on required variables for each model (drops rows with missing trial or other predictors); restrict RT models to correct trials (accuracy==1).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Primary confirmatory test is the single three-way interaction (Trial × Reward × NFC) on log RTs; no correction applied. Secondary models (accuracy or BAS-augmented) considered exploratory; report uncorrected p-values with clear labeling.

files.replication_data/sandra_replicate.csv: Trial-level dataset for the replication; includes SubjectID, logRT, accuracy, trial, rewardlevel, blocknumber, NFC, Stroopindex, BAS/BIS, etc.
files.replication_data/sandra_replicate_csv_co: Codebook describing variable definitions and coding (e.g., trial and rewardlevel contrast coding, blocknumber, NFC computation).
files.replication_data/analysis syntax_Sandra_: R analysis syntax showing focal models (e.g., summary(lmer(scale(logRT) ~ scale(NFC) * trial * rewardlevel + blocknumber + (1|SubjectID), data = sandra_replicate, subset = accuracy==1))). This is not a runnable script as-is because it assumes a preloaded data.frame named sandra_replicate.

codebase.notes: No executable script is provided. A new R script is needed to (a) read /app/data/sandra_replicate.csv, (b) run the focal lmer model, and (c) write outputs to /app/data. Ensure that all paths point to /app/data per run policy. The model formula is directly given in the analysis syntax PDF; only IO scaffolding is required.

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure sandra_replicate.csv is at /app/data. 2) In R, install.packages(c('lme4','lmerTest','readr','dplyr')) if needed. 3) Read CSV and run: lmer(scale(logRT) ~ scale(NFC) * trial * rewardlevel + blocknumber + (1|SubjectID), data=df[df$accuracy==1 & complete.cases(df[,c('logRT','trial','rewardlevel','blocknumber','NFC','SubjectID')]),]). 4) Record the coefficient and p-value for trial:rewardlevel:scale(NFC). 5) Optionally run glmer for accuracy with robust optimizer settings. 6) Save model summaries and fixed effects to /app/data outputs.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Primary: sign and significance of the Trial × Reward × NFC interaction coefficient compared to the original (expected positive, p≈0.008; original_paper.pdf Table 3; initial_details.txt; post_registration.json). Secondary: magnitude comparability (coefficient near 0.038 on log RT scale), and robustness when controlling for BAS (direction/significance retained).
